what_it_does: Bootstraps a React 18 application: it imports global CSS files, asserts that a DOM element with id "root" exists, and renders the App component inside React.StrictMode.
observations: Imports React and createRoot from react-dom/client
Loads three CSS files in order: ./tokens/paper.css, ./surface.css, ./rhm/rhm.css
Locates document.getElementById('root') and throws if it is missing
Renders <App /> wrapped in React.StrictMode using the new React 18 createRoot API
standouts: Explicit fail-loud guard for a missing #root element
CSS is loaded before the App component is imported/rendered
Uses React 18 createRoot rather than ReactDOM.render
conventions: Global style/theme loading before component mount
Defensive runtime assertion with a clear error message
StrictMode wrapping for highlighting potential problems
notes: This is the React 18 mount point: it loads global styles, asserts the DOM root exists, and renders the App under StrictMode.
questions: Is the CSS import order (tokens -> surface -> rhm) intentionally significant?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated beyond the 'fail loud' comment; the actual behavior matches a standard React 18 bootstrap.
apparent_themes: Layered design tokens and global styling
intention_signals: Set up a controlled, strict React 18 bootstrap with ordered global CSS and an immediate, clear failure if the expected DOM root is absent.
contribution: Provides the application's root mounting logic, defines the global CSS loading order, and adds a defensive guard that surfaces a missing root element at startup.
summary_for_embedding: React 18 entry file that imports global CSS, asserts a #root DOM element, and renders <App /> inside React.StrictMode.
intention_for_embedding: To bootstrap the UI by loading design tokens and styles before mounting the root component with strict-mode checks.
